mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, which translates to higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery shows labor market as key indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0%, it signals rising inflation risk, increasing the probability of future monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and a decrease in retail sales (RSAFS) over a 3-month period indicates a rapid deterioration in both the labor market and consumer spending, signaling an imminent recession and causing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.2 AND 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive 20-day forward return in over 60% of triggered events during any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) regime, positive surprises in industrial production (INDPRO) are interpreted as signs of a sustainable, non-inflationary recovery, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO monthly changes and SPX 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, often leading broader economic downturns and causing a rapid negative reaction in equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness preceded the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a highly accommodative environment for risk assets by lowering the discount rate on future earnings and encouraging a 'search for yield', providing a persistent tailwind for equities over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity performance during the post-GFC ZIRP era.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return of the S&P 500 during periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.0% is not statistically different from periods where FEDFUNDS > 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) and accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a stagflationary environment. This erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative equity returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in GDPC1 is decreasing AND Year-over-year % change in CPIAUCSL is increasing
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, a high absolute level of the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates significant economic slack. This allows the Federal Reserve to maintain an accommodative policy stance for longer, which supports equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed communications during 2010-2013 emphasized labor market slack.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is negative during periods when UNRATE is above 6.5% but below its cycle peak.
